questionText: My best friend is the person <u>__________</u> always supports me in difficult times.
explanation: On utilise 'who' pour remplacer une personne (my best friend) qui est le sujet du verbe 'supports'.

questionText: The laptop <u>__________</u> I bought last week has already crashed.
explanation: 'Which' est utilisé pour les choses ou les animaux (the laptop). C'est l'objet du verbe 'bought'.

questionText: Do you know the man <u>__________</u> car was stolen last night?
explanation: 'Whose' exprime la possession (la voiture de l'homme). Il se traduit par 'dont' ou 'duquel/de laquelle'.

questionText: The building <u>__________</u> was built in 1920 is now a museum.
explanation: 'Which' remplace 'the building' (une chose) qui est le sujet du verbe 'was built'.

questionText:
Complete the sentence with the correct relative pronoun:
The students, __________ had worked very hard, passed the exam with flying colors.
explanation: On utilise 'who' pour parler de personnes (the students) qui sont le sujet du verbe 'had worked'.

options: That's the girl who won the competition.
I have a friend whose father is a doctor.
The book which I read it was fascinating.
The cake which she baked was delicious.
explanation: La phrase 'The book which I read it was fascinating' est incorrecte car l'objet 'it' est redondant. Le pronom relatif 'which' remplace déjà l'objet 'the book'. On dit : 'The book which I read was fascinating'.

questionText: My mother, <u>__________</u> is a nurse, works at the Central Hospital of Yaoundé.
explanation: 'Who' remplace 'my mother' (une personne) et est le sujet du verbe 'is'.

questionText: The film <u>__________</u> we saw last night was very boring.
explanation: 'Which' remplace 'the film' (une chose) et est l'objet du verbe 'saw'. On pourrait aussi utiliser 'that'.

questionText: This is the writer <u>__________</u> novels have sold millions of copies worldwide.
explanation: 'Whose' indique la possession : les romans <u>de cet écrivain</u>. C'est le pronom possessif relatif.

questionText:
Combine the two sentences using the correct relative pronoun:
'The house is for sale. Its roof was damaged in the storm.'
options: The house which roof was damaged in the storm is for sale.
The house whose roof was damaged in the storm is for sale.
The house who roof was damaged in the storm is for sale.
The house whom roof was damaged in the storm is for sale.
explanation: Le pronom 'whose' est le seul possible ici car il relie la maison à son toit (relation de possession). 'Whose' signifie 'of which' pour les objets. La phrase correcte est : 'The house whose roof was damaged in the storm is for sale.'
